metadata.startup-script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

apt-get update
apt-get install -y \
  apt-transport-https \
  ca-certificates \
  curl \
  gnupg \
  jq \
  lsb-release \
  nfs-common \
  postgresql-client 

install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings
curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc
chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc

tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.sources <<EOF
Types: deb
URIs: https://download.docker.com/linux/ubuntu
Suites: $(. /etc/os-release && echo "${UBUNTU_CODENAME:-$VERSION_CODENAME}")
Components: stable
Signed-By: /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc
EOF

apt-get update
apt-get install docker-ce -y \
 docker-ce-cli \
 containerd.io \
 docker-buildx-plugin \
 docker-compose-plugin

if ! command -v snap >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  apt-get install -y snapd
fi

snap install google-cloud-cli --classic || true
snap install kubectl --classic || true

echo "Bastion bootstrap complete"

values.name: nginx-ingress
values:
controller:
  service:
    type: ClusterIP
    annotations:
      cloud.google.com/neg: '{"exposed_ports": {"80":{"name": "ingress-nginx-80-neg-http"}}}'
  config:
    use-forwarded-headers: "true"

values.name: gcp-secret-store
values.yaml_body_parsed:
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: SecretStore
metadata:
  name: gcp-secret-store
  namespace: default
spec:
  provider:
    gcpsm:
      auth:
        workloadIdentity:
          clusterLocation: europe-west2-a
          clusterName: mycompany-dev-gke
          serviceAccountRef:
            name: external-secrets-sa
            namespace: external-secrets
      projectID: mycompany-project-123456

values.name: postgres
values.yaml_body_parsed:
apiVersion: external-secrets.io/v1beta1
kind: ExternalSecret
metadata:
  name: postgres
  namespace: default
spec:
  data:
  - remoteRef:
      key: mycompany-dev-db-user
    secretKey: pguser
  - remoteRef:
      key: mycompany-dev-db-password
    secretKey: pgpass
  - remoteRef:
      key: mycompany-dev-db-name
    secretKey: pgdb
  refreshInterval: 1h
  secretStoreRef:
    kind: SecretStore
    name: gcp-secret-store
  target:
    creationPolicy: Owner
    name: postgres

metadata.startup-script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

export DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

DISK_DEVICE="/dev/disk/by-id/google-nfs-data"
MOUNT_POINT="/srv/nfs/shared"
CLIENT_CIDR="${gke_cluster_ipv4_cidr}"

apt-get update
apt-get install -y nfs-kernel-server

mkdir -p "${MOUNT_POINT}"

if ! blkid "${DISK_DEVICE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  mkfs.ext4 -F "${DISK_DEVICE}"
fi

grep -q "${DISK_DEVICE}" /etc/fstab || echo "${DISK_DEVICE} ${MOUNT_POINT} ext4 defaults,nofail 0 2" >> /etc/fstab
mount -a

mkdir -p "${MOUNT_POINT}"
chown -R nobody:nogroup "${MOUNT_POINT}"
chmod 0770 "${MOUNT_POINT}"

cat >/etc/exports <<EOF
${MOUNT_POINT} ${CLIENT_CIDR}(rw,sync,no_subtree_check,no_root_squash)
EOF

exportfs -ra
systemctl enable nfs-kernel-server
systemctl restart nfs-kernel-server

echo "NFS bootstrap complete"

artifact_registry_repository_url.description: Base Artifact Registry URL for pushing and pulling container images.

repository_name.description: Full Artifact Registry repository resource name.

gke_lb_target_tag.description: Network tag applied to GKE nodes that should receive LB traffic.

helm_readiness.description: a BS output so LBS module doesn't try to MAP NEG before its ready

authorized_networks.description: Map of CIDR => display name for GKE master authorized networks.

company.description: Company or project prefix used for naming.

db_name_secret_name.description: Secret Manager secret name for DB name.

db_password_secret_name.description: Secret Manager secret name for DB password.

db_user_secret_name.description: Secret Manager secret name for DB username.

gke_cloudsql_sa.description: Google service account email used by Cloud SQL access workloads in GKE.

gke_external_secrets_sa.description: Google service account email used by External Secrets Operator.

gke_lb_target_tag.description: Network tag applied to GKE nodes that should receive LB traffic.

gke_machine_type.description: Machine type for GKE worker nodes.

gke_master_ipv4_cidr.description: CIDR for the private GKE control plane.

gke_num_nodes.description: Initial number of nodes in the GKE node pool.

network_id.description: VPC network ID or self link.

privatenetwork_subnet.description: Self link or identifier of the private subnet used by GKE.

vm_ip_cidr.description: CIDR allowed to reach the private control plane.

bastion_service_account.description: Service account attached to the bastion VM.

bitbucket_workload_identity_provider.description: Full resource name of the Bitbucket WIF provider

gke_sql_service_account.description: The SA used for GKE to access CloudSQL

nfs_service_account.description: The SA used for NFS instance

bastion_access_members.description: Users or groups allowed to access the bastion via IAP and OS Login.

frontend_access_members.description: Users or groups that should receive the frontend custom role.

cloudrun_hosts.description: Hostnames routed to the Cloud Run backend.

gke_hosts.description: Hostnames routed to the GKE backend.

lb_certificate_domains.description: Domains attached to the Google-managed certificate.

lb_ip.description: Reserved public IP for the load balancer.

network_id.description: VPC network ID or self link.

privatenetwork_subnet.description: Private subnet used by load-balanced backends.

network_id.description: The ID of the VPC being created

network_name.description: The name of the VPC being created

enabled_api_identities.description: Enabled API identities in the project

project_id.description: The GCP project you want to enable APIs on

activate_api_identities.description:
The list of service identities (Google Managed service account for the API) to force-create for the project (e.g. in order to grant additional roles).
    APIs in this list will automatically be appended to `activate_apis`.
    Not including the API in this list will follow the default behaviour for identity creation (which is usually when the first resource using the API is created).
    Any roles (e.g. service agent role) must be explicitly listed. See https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/understanding-roles#service-agent-roles-roles for a list of related roles.

activate_apis.description: The list of apis to activate within the project

disable_dependent_services.description: Whether services that are enabled and which depend on this service should also be disabled when this service is destroyed. https://www.terraform.io/docs/providers/google/r/google_project_service.html#disable_dependent_services

disable_services_on_destroy.description: Whether project services will be disabled when the resources are destroyed. https://www.terraform.io/docs/providers/google/r/google_project_service.html#disable_on_destroy

enable_apis.description: Whether to actually enable the APIs. If false, this module is a no-op.

project_id.description: The GCP project you want to enable APIs on

db_name_secret_id.description: Secret Manager secret id for DB name

db_name_secret_name.description: Secret Manager name for DB name

db_password_secret_id.description: Secret Manager secret id for DB password

db_password_secret_name.description: Secret Manager name for DB password

db_user_secret_id.description: Secret Manager secret id for DB username

db_user_secret_name.description: Secret Manager name for DB username

db_disk_size.description: Cloud SQL disk size in GB.

network_id.description: VPC network ID or self link used for private service networking.

artifact_registry_repository_id.description: Artifact Registry repository ID for container images.

authorized_networks.description: Map of authorized CIDR blocks to display names for GKE master authorized networks.

bastion_access_members.description: Users or groups allowed to access the bastion through IAP and OS Login. Example: user:alice@example.com or group:platform@example.com

bastion_internal_ip.description: Static internal IP address assigned to the bastion VM.

bastion_machine_type.description: Machine type for the bastion VM.

bitbucket_workspace.description: Bitbucket workspace slug used for Workload Identity Federation.

bitbucket_workspace_uuid.description: Bitbucket workspace UUID used in OIDC trust conditions.

cloudrun_hosts.description: Hostnames routed to the Cloud Run backend.

company.description: Short company or project prefix used in resource naming.

db_availability_type.description: Cloud SQL availability type. Use REGIONAL for HA or ZONAL for single-zone.

db_deletion.description: Whether deletion protection is enabled for database resources.

db_disk_size.description: Cloud SQL disk size in GB.

db_disk_type.description: Cloud SQL disk type. Valid values are PD_SSD or PD_HDD.

db_point_recovery.description: Whether Cloud SQL point-in-time recovery is enabled. Required for HA/REGIONAL setups.

db_tier.description: Cloud SQL machine tier, for example db-custom-2-7680.

description.description: Description applied to network resources where supported.

frontend_access_members.description: Users or groups that should receive the frontend custom role.

gke_cluster_ipv4_cidr.description: CIDR block used for GKE pod IPs.

gke_hosts.description: Hostnames routed to the GKE backend.

gke_lb_target_tag.description: Network tag applied to GKE nodes that should receive LB traffic.

gke_machine_type.description: Machine type used by GKE worker nodes.

gke_master_ipv4_cidr.description: CIDR block used for the private GKE control plane endpoint.

gke_num_nodes.description: Initial number of nodes in the GKE node pool.

gke_services_ipv4_cidr.description: CIDR block used for GKE service IPs.

lb_certificate_domains.description: Domains attached to the managed LB certificate.

max_node_count.description: Maximum number of nodes for GKE autoscaling.

min_node_count.description: Minimum number of nodes for GKE autoscaling.

nfs_data_disk_size_gb.description: Persistent data disk size in GB for the NFS VM.

nfs_data_disk_type.description: Persistent data disk type for the NFS VM.

nfs_internal_ip.description: Static internal IP address assigned to the NFS VM.

nfs_machine_type.description: Machine type for the NFS VM.

pri_subnet_cidr.description: Primary subnet CIDR block for the private VPC subnet.

pri_vpc_peering_address.description: CIDR range reserved for Private Service Access / VPC peering.

project.description: GCP project ID where resources will be created.

region.description: Primary GCP region for regional resources.

routing_mode.description: VPC routing mode. Valid values are REGIONAL or GLOBAL.

use_filestore.description: When true, provision Filestore. When false, provision the NFS VM.

vm_ip_cidr.description: CIDR block allowed to access the bastion or private control plane where applicable.

zone.description: Primary GCP zone for zonal resources.
